content.body:
# IT Management System — Architecture Initialization

Initial setup of a scalable IT Management System using a modular monorepo architecture, designed for maintainability, performance, and clear service boundaries.

## Architecture Highlights
- Modular Monorepo (Next.js 14 + Fastify services)
- Built for scalability and service isolation
- Domain-driven module structure

## Tech Stack
- **Frontend:** Next.js 14, React, Tailwind CSS 4
- **Backend:** Fastify
- **Database:** PostgreSQL 16
- **ORM:** Drizzle
- **Queue:** BullMQ
- **Testing:** Vitest (JSDOM)
- **Tooling:** TypeScript (strict), Husky

## Core Modules
- **Auth** – Authentication & authorization
- **Users** – User management
- **Assets** – Asset tracking
- **Tickets** – Issue tracking system
- **Reports** – Analytics & reporting
- **Monitoring** – System health & logging

## Design Principles
- Append-only database history
- Immutable event-driven patterns
- Thin routes, Fat services
- Repository pattern
- Clear separation of concerns

## Project Structure
- `apps/` – Frontend applications
- `services/` – Backend services
- `packages/` – Shared logic and types
- `system-design/` – Architecture and planning docs

## Documentation
- `README_ARCHITECTURE.md` – Overview & onboarding
- `system-design/architecture_spec.md` – Technical specs
- `system-design/planning.md` – Roadmap and planning

## Configuration
- `package.json` – Dependencies and scripts
- `tsconfig.json` – Strict TypeScript setup
- `vite.config.ts` – Vitest + JSDOM config

## Summary
This commit establishes the foundation of the IT Management System, including architecture, standards, documentation, and core configuration—ready for development and team collaboration.
content.title: docs: System design planning

content.body:
### Tasks
- [ ] Initialize backend project, frontend project, linter, formatter, CI config.
- [ ] Add `docker-compose.yml` for DB + app for local dev.
- [ ] Wire pre-commit hooks: lint, typecheck, test.

### Acceptance Criteria
- `docker compose up` brings up DB + empty API + empty frontend.
- CI runs lint + typecheck + tests on PR and blocks on failure.
- README documents local setup in ≤ 5 commands.
content.title: Story 0.1 — Repo scaffolding & tooling

content.body:
### Tasks
- [ ] JWT issue/verify helpers.
- [ ] Role-based middleware: `admin`, `it_staff`, `end_user`.
- [ ] Shared error envelope `{ error: { code, message } }`.

### Acceptance Criteria
- Middleware rejects missing/expired/invalid tokens with 401.
- RBAC denies unauthorized roles with 403.
- Unit tests cover happy path + each rejection case.
content.title: Story 0.3 — Shared auth middleware & RBAC

content.body:
### Tasks
- [ ] `POST /api/v1/auth/login` → returns JWT on valid credentials, 401 otherwise.
- [ ] Login page with form, error display, redirect to dashboard on success.
- [ ] Rate-limit login attempts (per IP + per username).

### Acceptance Criteria
- Valid creds → 200 + token + redirect to `/dashboard`.
- Invalid creds → inline error "Invalid username or password" (no user enumeration).
- 5 failed attempts in 1 min → 429.
- Passwords stored hashed (bcrypt/argon2), never logged.
content.title: Story 1.1 — Login endpoint + UI

content.body:
### Tasks
- [ ] Choose migration tool; create initial migration with tables: `users`, `roles`, `assets`, `asset_assignments`, `tickets`, `ticket_events`, `devices`, `device_status_log`, `alerts`, `audit_log`.
- [ ] Seed script with one admin user.

### Acceptance Criteria
- `migrate up` / `migrate down` both succeed on empty DB.
- Seed produces a loginable admin.
- ER diagram committed to `docs/erd.md`.
content.title: Story 0.2 — Database schema & migration framework

content.body:
### Tasks
- [ ] Authenticated layout with nav: Users, Assets, Tickets, Reports, Monitoring, Logout.
- [ ] Hide nav items by role.

### Acceptance Criteria
- Unauthenticated users hitting `/dashboard` are redirected to `/login`.
- End users do not see User Management or Reports nav items.
- Logout clears token and redirects to `/login`.
content.title: Story 1.2 — Dashboard shell & navigation

content.body:
### Tasks
- [ ] Define `NotificationService` interface (email + in-app).
- [ ] Provide a no-op + console implementation for dev.

### Acceptance Criteria
- Interface documented in `shared/notifications/README.md`.
- Tickets and Monitoring epics can import it without pulling a concrete implementation.
content.title: Story 0.4 — Shared notification service interface

content.body:
### Tasks
- [ ] `POST /api/v1/auth/logout` → invalidates refresh token / clears session.

### Acceptance Criteria
- After logout, prior token cannot access protected routes.
- Audit log entry written.
content.title: Story 1.3 — Logout endpoint

content.body:
### Tasks
- [ ] Users table with search, filter by role, create/edit modals, delete confirm.

### Acceptance Criteria
- All CRUD actions from UI reflect in DB within one network round trip.
- Form validation matches API validation (required fields, email format).
- Destructive actions require confirmation.
content.title: Story 2.2 — User management UI

content.body:
### Tasks
- [ ] Write audit log row on create/update/delete with actor, target, before/after.

### Acceptance Criteria
- Every mutation produces exactly one audit row.
- Audit rows are queryable by target user id.
content.title: Story 2.3 — Audit logging for user changes

items.title: Story 2.3 — Audit logging for user changes

content.body:
### Tasks
- [ ] `GET/POST/PATCH/DELETE /api/v1/assets` with fields: tag, type, serial, status, purchase date, warranty.

### Acceptance Criteria
- Asset tag is unique; duplicate returns 409.
- Status is a bounded enum (`in_stock`, `assigned`, `repair`, `retired`).
- IT staff + admin can mutate; end users read-only on assets assigned to them.
content.title: Story 3.1 — CRUD assets (API)

content.body:
### Tasks
- [ ] `GET/POST/PATCH/DELETE /api/v1/users` with pagination + search.
- [ ] Enforce admin-only access.

### Acceptance Criteria
- Admin can create, list (paginated), update, soft-delete users.
- Non-admin receives 403.
- Email uniqueness enforced; duplicate returns 409.
- Soft-deleted users cannot log in.
content.title: Story 2.1 — CRUD users (API)

content.body:
### Tasks
- [ ] `POST /api/v1/assets/:id/assign` body `{ userId }`; `POST /api/v1/assets/:id/unassign`.
- [ ] Create `asset_assignments` history row on each change.

### Acceptance Criteria
- Assigning an already-assigned asset returns 409 unless `force=true`.
- Assignment history preserved across reassignments.
- Asset status transitions to `assigned` / back to `in_stock` automatically.
content.title: Story 3.2 — Assign / unassign asset to user

items.title: Story 3.2 — Assign / unassign asset to user

content.body:
### Tasks
- [ ] Assets table, create/edit form, assign dialog with user search.

### Acceptance Criteria
- UI shows current holder and full assignment history.
- Assign dialog searches users via API (debounced).
- CSV import stub is out of scope for this epic.
content.title: Story 3.3 — Asset management UI

content.body:
### Tasks
- [ ] `POST /api/v1/tickets` with fields: title, description, priority, category, optional asset id.
- [ ] Submit form for end users.

### Acceptance Criteria
- Ticket persists with `status=open`, `createdBy=currentUser`.
- On create, `NotificationService.notify('it_staff', …)` fires exactly once.
- Validation: title ≤ 120 chars, description required.
content.title: Story 4.1 — Submit ticket (API + UI)

content.body:
### Tasks
- [ ] `GET /api/v1/tickets` (filter by status, assignee, priority, mine).
- [ ] `PATCH /api/v1/tickets/:id` for status/assignee/comment.
- [ ] Ticket detail page with event timeline.

### Acceptance Criteria
- End users see only their own tickets; IT staff see all.
- Every status/assignee change writes a `ticket_events` row.
- Closing a ticket requires a resolution note.
content.title: Story 4.2 — View & update ticket

content.body:
### Tasks
- [ ] Hook into `NotificationService` on create and on reassignment.

### Acceptance Criteria
- New ticket → notification to all `it_staff` role members (or assigned group).
- Reassignment → notification to new assignee only.
- Notification failures are logged but do not fail the request.
content.title: Story 4.3 — Notify IT staff on new/updated tickets

items.title: Story 4.3 — Notify IT staff on new/updated tickets

content.body:
### Tasks
- [ ] Implement reports: tickets by status, tickets by resolution time, assets by status, user activity.
- [ ] Date range + role filter parameters.

### Acceptance Criteria
- Each report returns JSON with consistent envelope `{ columns, rows, generatedAt }`.
- Queries execute under 2s on seed data set (≥ 10k rows).
content.title: Story 5.1 — Report definitions

content.body:
### Tasks
- [ ] Reports page listing available reports; parameter form; table + simple chart render.

### Acceptance Criteria
- Selecting a report and submitting parameters renders results without full page reload.
- Empty results show a clear "No data" state, not a blank table.
content.title: Story 5.2 — Report UI (display)

content.body:
### Tasks
- [ ] `GET /api/v1/reports/:id/export?format=csv|pdf`.

### Acceptance Criteria
- CSV opens cleanly in Excel/Sheets (UTF-8 BOM, quoted fields).
- PDF includes title, parameters, generated timestamp.
- Only admin + it_staff can export.
content.title: Story 5.3 — Export (CSV + PDF)

content.body:
### Tasks
- [ ] Background worker polls each device per its interval (ICMP or TCP port check).
- [ ] Write `device_status_log` row per check.

### Acceptance Criteria
- Worker survives restart (resumes from schedule, no duplicate checks).
- Failing check transitions device to `down` after N consecutive failures (configurable, default 3).
- Recovery transitions back to `up` on first success.
content.title: Story 6.2 — Health check worker

content.body:
### Tasks
- [ ] `GET/POST/PATCH/DELETE /api/v1/devices` with host/ip, type, check interval.

### Acceptance Criteria
- IP/host uniqueness enforced.
- Admin-only mutations; it_staff read-only.
content.title: Story 6.1 — Device registry

content.body:
### Tasks
- [ ] On transition to `down`, call `NotificationService` once.
- [ ] Create row in `alerts` with device, first-seen, status.
- [ ] On recovery, append resolution timestamp.

### Acceptance Criteria
- No duplicate alerts for the same ongoing incident.
- Alert contains device identifier, time, and last error.
- Alerts page lists open + recent alerts, filterable by device.
content.title: Story 6.3 — Alerting on detected issue

content.body:
### Tasks
- [ ] Dashboard widget: counts of up/down devices + latest alerts.

### Acceptance Criteria
- Widget refreshes every 30s without full page reload.
- Clicking a device navigates to its detail/status history.
content.title: Story 6.4 — Monitoring dashboard widget

content.body:
### Tasks
- [ ] Implement E2E smoke tests for the main flow.

### Acceptance Criteria
- Scripted flow: login → create ticket → assign asset → generate report → logout runs green in CI.
content.title: Story 7.1 — E2E smoke tests

content.body:
### Tasks
- [ ] Implement structured logs, health endpoints, and basic metrics.

### Acceptance Criteria
- Structured logs with request id; `/healthz` and `/readyz` endpoints; basic metrics (request count, latency, error rate).
content.title: Story 7.2 — Observability

content.body:
### Tasks
- [ ] Dependency audit, CSRF protection, security headers, env secrets.

### Acceptance Criteria
- Dependency audit clean; CSRF protection on state-changing forms; security headers set; secrets pulled from env, not code.
content.title: Story 7.3 — Security hardening pass

content.body:
# API Backend Project Plan - IT Management System

This plan outlines the implementation of the modular API backend for the IT Management System, following the architecture and tech stack defined in `CLAUDE.md`.

## 🏗 Tech Stack & Architecture
- **Runtime**: Node.js 20 LTS
- **Language**: TypeScript (Strict)
- **Framework**: Fastify (Schema-first validation)
- **ORM**: Drizzle ORM
- **Database**: PostgreSQL 16
- **Monorepo Manager**: pnpm workspaces
- **Background Jobs**: BullMQ on Redis
- **Auth**: JWT (Access + Refresh tokens), argon2 hashing
- **Testing**: Vitest (Unit), Supertest (Integration)
- **API Style**: RESTful JSON API (`/api/v1/...`)

---

## 📅 Epic Breakdown & Implementation Roadmap

### Epic 0: Foundation & Shared Infrastructure (Blocking)
*Goal: Setup the monorepo, shared modules, and core utilities.*

- **T-0.1: Monorepo Scaffolding**
  - Initialize pnpm workspace.
  - Setup root `package.json`, `tsconfig.json`, ESLint, and Prettier.
  - Create directory structure: `apps/web`, `services/`, `shared/`.
  - Configure `docker-compose.yml` for PostgreSQL and Redis.
- **T-0.2: Shared Database & Migrations**
  - Initialize Drizzle in `shared/db`.
  - Define initial schema (Users, Roles, Assets, Tickets, etc.).
  - Setup migration scripts and a seed script for an admin user.
- **T-0.3: Shared Auth & RBAC Middleware**
  - Implement JWT helpers (sign/verify) in `shared/auth`.
  - Create Fastify middleware for role-based access control (`admin`, `it_staff`, `end_user`).
- **T-0.4: Shared Notification Interface**
  - Define `NotificationService` interface in `shared/notifications`.
  - Implement a `ConsoleNotificationProvider` for development.

### Epic 1: Authentication & Session Management
- **T-1.1: Login API**
  - `POST /api/v1/auth/login`: Validate credentials, issue tokens, argon2 verification.
  - Rate limiting (per IP/username).
- **T-1.2: Refresh & Logout**
  - `POST /api/v1/auth/refresh`: Issue new access token using refresh token.
  - `POST /api/v1/auth/logout`: Invalidate refresh token.

### Epic 2: User Management (Admin Only)
- **T-2.1: User CRUD API**
  - `GET /api/v1/users`: Paginated list with search/filter.
  - `POST /api/v1/users`: Create user with role assignment.
  - `PATCH /api/v1/users/:id`: Update user details.
  - `DELETE /api/v1/users/:id`: Soft delete.
- **T-2.2: Audit Logging**
  - Implement a shared audit subscriber to log all user mutations.

### Epic 3: Asset Management
- **T-3.1: Asset CRUD API**
  - `GET/POST/PATCH/DELETE /api/v1/assets`.
  - Fields: tag, type, serial, status, purchase date, warranty.
- **T-3.2: Asset Assignment**
  - `POST /api/v1/assets/:id/assign`: Link asset to a user.
  - `POST /api/v1/assets/:id/unassign`: Return asset to stock.
  - Maintain assignment history in `asset_assignments` table.

### Epic 4: Ticketing System
- **T-4.1: Ticket Submission & Lifecycle**
  - `POST /api/v1/tickets`: End-user submission.
  - `GET /api/v1/tickets`: Filtered list (Admin/IT see all, Users see own).
  - `PATCH /api/v1/tickets/:id`: Update status, assignee, or add comments.
- **T-4.2: IT Notifications**
  - Integration with `NotificationService` to alert IT staff on new/updated tickets.

### Epic 5: Reports & Analytics
- **T-5.1: Analytics Endpoints**
  - Generate data for: tickets by status, resolution times, asset utilization.
  - Support date range filtering.
- **T-5.2: Export Functionality**
  - `GET /api/v1/reports/:id/export?format=csv|pdf`.

### Epic 6: Network Monitoring & Alerts
- **T-6.1: Device Registry API**
  - CRUD for monitored network devices (host/IP, check interval).
- **T-6.2: Health Check Worker**
  - Implementation of BullMQ worker to poll devices (ICMP/TCP).
  - Log results to `device_status_log`.
- **T-6.3: Incident Alerting**
  - Automatic creation of `alerts` when a device goes down.
  - Notify IT staff via `NotificationService`.

---

## 🧪 Testing & Quality Assurance
- **Unit Tests**: Business logic in `services/<module>/service.ts`.
- **Integration Tests**: API endpoints using Supertest.
- **CI/CD**: GitHub Actions pipeline for linting, typechecking, and testing.

## 🚀 Execution Strategy
1. **Initialize Epic 0** (Single agent to avoid conflicts).
2. **Sequential Implementation of Epics 1 & 2**.
3. **Parallel Implementation** of Epics 3-6 once foundations are solid.
content.title: API Architecture Design & Documentation
